spec_match.rationale: Pairs visual ordinals (1st/2nd) with spelled-out alt (first/second) via custom counter-style and the alt-text content segment.

interactive.rationale: Interactive markers count 2 — minimal. No controls to flip styles, add items, or hear the readout.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Blurb promises ordinal visual + spelled alt; the page does that.

edge_cases.rationale: Doesn't address languages beyond English (Spanish ordinals differ, German has gendered forms), nor numbers beyond ~10 where 'eleventh' / 'twelfth' get tricky in CSS @counter-style.

references.rationale: Includes the correct chromestatus.com/feature/5185442420621312 link in see-also.

openQuestions.title: Add interactivity
openQuestions.detail: Visitor should be able to add/remove list items, toggle between ordinal-only and spelled-out display, and see the alt-text readout updating. Currently 2 interactive markers means it's nearly a static card.

openQuestions.title: Localisation
openQuestions.detail: Show the same pattern in another language — the @counter-style descriptor work multiplies the cardinality. A toggle between English and Spanish ordinals would demonstrate the scope.

summary: A clean idea but the lightest interactive presence of the five. Add visitor-driven add/remove and a localisation toggle.
